pre_game.message: Hey, I'm J. It's a bit crowded in here, isn't it? What brings you to a game like this?
Hey J, I'm F. It really is. Honestly, I'm just here for the prize money—got some people back home I need to take care of. What about you?
I'm in the same boat, F. I've got people counting on me back home too, so I'm pretty determined to walk away with that prize money.
I hear you. It definitely adds some pressure when it's not just about yourself. How many people are you looking out for?
Three people—my parents and my younger brother. It really does up the stakes. How many are you playing for?
For me, it's my parents and my younger sister; they've sacrificed a lot, so I really need to make this happen for them.
I totally get that. It’s a lot of weight on your shoulders when you want to pay them back for everything they've done.
It definitely is, but it's the best motivation I could have. Are you nervous about the game itself starting soon?
A little, mostly because we don't know what to expect yet. But the goal keeps me focused. How about you?
Same here. I'm trying to stay sharp and not let the uncertainty get to me. We're both here for the right reasons, so let's just hope for the best.
Definitely. It's good to know someone else here has their head on straight. Good luck out there, F.
Thanks, J. Good luck to you too—hope things work out for your family.

post_game.message: I took $10,000 because it was the fair share for all ten of us. Thanks for doing the same!
I agree, taking the equal share was the only fair way to handle it. I'm glad we both chose to do so.
